species: BIG-Syc 13-strain infant-gut synthetic community (B. infantis, B. bifidum, B. breve, Bacteroides fragilis/vulgatus/ovatus, Veillonella parvula, Ruminococcus gnavus, E. coli

up.method: reported concentration only; >90% degraded, no specific rate given

sec.method: HPLC, main product, no numeric value in text/table
HPLC, no numeric value in text/table

growth_records.cite: Ioannou et al. Resource sharing of an infant gut microbiota synthetic community in combinations of human milk oligosaccharides. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae209. [PMC11542058]
growth_records.snippet: The working volume was 300 ml ... A flow rate of 12.5 ml/h ensured complete refresh of the medium within 24 h ... Basal medium with 0.4% w/v 4HMO or 5HMO mix was used ... pH ... maintained at 6
growth_records.notes: Continuous (chemostat) co-culture. Dilution rate D = flow/volume = 12.5 mL/h / 300 mL = 0.04167 /h; consistent with stated 24 h full refresh (300/12.5=24 h). At steady state community mu = D = 0.0417 /h; doubling time = ln2/D = 0.6931/0.04167 = 16.6 h. This is a whole-community rate, NOT a per-strain measurement. Basal medium composition given only by reference [ref 20] + 1 g/L yeast extract, minus Na2S -> composition marked not fully stated. Metabolite (acetate/propionate/etc.) concentrations reported only as bar plots in Fig. 2 (mM), no extractable numeric values, so secretion rates left null.
